raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.3,"R1.3":0.0,"R1.4":0.0,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.0,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.1":"Contribution claim and gap statement exist but are buried in hedged run-on prose; an 'improve' delegation plausibly includes surfacing and sharpening them.","R1.5":"The four-paragraph arc meanders — gap, scattered results, key comparison, conclusion — with no clear signposting; structural tightening is a natural part of 'improve this draft'.","R1.6":"Load-bearing: the draft has pervasive sentence-level pathologies — cascading relative clauses, excessive hedges ('which is something we believe represents…', 'which seems to suggest'), and filler qualifiers ('going forward', 'in this study') — that any meaningful improvement must address."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should improvements stay at the sentence level (fixing run-ons, stripping hedging language, removing filler), or should they also include reconceptualizing the contribution framing and reshaping the paragraph structure?"}

rationale.R1.1: Contribution claim and gap statement exist but are buried in hedged run-on prose; an 'improve' delegation plausibly includes surfacing and sharpening them.
rationale.R1.5: The four-paragraph arc meanders — gap, scattered results, key comparison, conclusion — with no clear signposting; structural tightening is a natural part of 'improve this draft'.
rationale.R1.6: Load-bearing: the draft has pervasive sentence-level pathologies — cascading relative clauses, excessive hedges ('which is something we believe represents…', 'which seems to suggest'), and filler qualifiers ('going forward', 'in this study') — that any meaningful improvement must address.

parsed.clarification_question: Should improvements stay at the sentence level (fixing run-ons, stripping hedging language, removing filler), or should they also include reconceptualizing the contribution framing and reshaping the paragraph structure?
